reason: prod/platform/platform-core was missing NOTIFICATIONS_API_KEY entirely — confirmed via key-listing before write (staging had 29 keys, prod had 25, missing NOTIFICATIONS_API_KEY/SCHEDULER_SERVICE_API_KEY/SCHEDULER_WEBHOOK_SECRET/SLACK_CONNECT_INTERNAL_SECRET). This snapshot covers the first of the four: copied staging's value verbatim into prod so notifications work end-to-end after the prod promote.
change_kind: aws-secrets-manager put-secret-value (read-modify-write, added missing key, all 25 existing keys preserved) + github-actions-secret-add

source_of_truth.note: Value NOT captured in this file — write-only discipline. The sha256 prefix lets a future audit confirm prod + GitHub secret still match this source without exposing either value.

targets_written.note: Read-modify-write: fetched prod's current 25-key payload, added NOTIFICATIONS_API_KEY via jq merge, wrote back. All 25 pre-existing keys unchanged.

targets_written.name: NOTIFICATIONS_API_KEY
targets_written.consumed_by: .github/workflows/deploy.yml -> scripts/sync-pulumi-config.sh -> pulumi config -> ECS task env
targets_written.must_match: prod/platform/platform-core NOTIFICATIONS_API_KEY — mismatch = notifications auth failures between slack-server and platform-core in prod

rollback: AWS: aws secretsmanager put-secret-value --secret-id prod/platform/platform-core --region us-east-1 --profile brighthive-production --secret-string <full-25-key-payload-without-NOTIFICATIONS_API_KEY>  (removes the key by omission, restoring pre-2026-07-21 state).
